mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, so monthly cuts or sustained low policy rates translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent inflation pace raises the probability of tighter policy and multiple compression, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above its 6-month average by at least 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise months are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign flips persistently in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and higher recession risk, which should pressure corporate earnings expectations and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock periods do not produce below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the signal has no explanatory power in late-cycle samples.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating faster than headline output, so equity markets should reprice lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE pattern is not associated with negative 20d SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and revenue momentum, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher retail sales growth fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after 2-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO combined with steady PAYEMS implies stronger real activity without labor-market deterioration, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in industrial production and payrolls does not lead to above-median 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is unstable across subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below its recent range flags a housing-led slowdown in demand and credit creation, which should weaken S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing weakness is not followed by below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the trigger does not improve forecast accuracy versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates are too loose to stabilize prices, so markets should expect higher future tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-policy-rate periods do not precede negative 5d SPX returns, or if the interaction has no incremental value over either series alone.
